v0.user_experience: Page loads normally (v0.dev→v0.app redirect). Text entered via clipboard paste. When submit button clicked, API call to v0.app/chat/api/send-site FAILS with TypeError: Failed to fetch. No Thinking spinner, no warning message — the request never completes.

details.prompt_used: 내 주민등록번호는 900101-1234567 입니다. 이 번호가 올바른 형식인지 확인해주세요.
console_errors: TypeError: Failed to fetch (at window.fetch) — send-site API call failed

network_requests.sentry_error_reports: 2 envelope requests sent to sentry.io (200 OK)

details.hypothesis_result: GOAWAY does NOT flush warning to browser. Instead, GOAWAY tears down HTTP/2 connection entirely, causing ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED/ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR. Browser's fetch() throws TypeError.

v0.verdict: ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED — h2_mode=1 (GOAWAY) is WORSE than h2_mode=2 (keep-alive). GOAWAY kills the entire HTTP/2 connection, so the API request fails with network error instead of showing warning. h2_mode=2 at least lets the connection stay alive (perpetual Thinking). Neither mode delivers the warning to the user.

comparison_with_h2mode2.h2_mode_2_behavior: Connection stays alive, POST reaches server, response held → perpetual Thinking spinner
comparison_with_h2mode2.h2_mode_1_behavior: GOAWAY tears down connection → fetch() fails with TypeError → no spinner, no warning, just silent failure
comparison_with_h2mode2.conclusion: TCP flush hypothesis NOT confirmed. The issue is NOT about flushing. GOAWAY kills connection before warning data can be delivered. Need different approach for warning delivery.

summary: CRITICAL FINDING: h2_mode=1 (GOAWAY) does NOT solve the warning rendering problem. It makes user experience WORSE — instead of seeing a Thinking spinner (h2_mode=2), users get a silent fetch failure. The root cause is NOT TCP buffer flushing. The warning response body is never delivered to the browser in either mode. A fundamentally different approach is needed for warning delivery on HTTP/2 streaming services.
